resources.string: This will erase all your customization and settings, returning to factory defaults.

• Tap a button to play its sound.

• Long-press a button, or choose "Customize sounds" from the menu, to enter edit mode and change its icon, image, text, sound and volume.

• You can use an icon, pick and crop your own image, or type custom text for the button (text auto-shrinks to fit, or gets truncated with an ellipsis if it still doesn't fit).

• When picking a button's sound you can record it with the microphone, import it from your device, search for it online, or use one of the built-in ones. While recording you can listen to the result, trim the start or end to remove silence, re-record, and you must give it a name before saving.

• "Search sound online" lets you preview each result before downloading it, sort them by duration or relevance, and load more results if there are any; downloading one goes through the same trim step before saving. Requires an internet connection.

• In Settings you can set an alternative server of your own for those downloads; if it doesn't respond, the built-in method is used automatically.

• Any own sound (recorded, imported or downloaded online) can be re-trimmed, and renamed, later with the pencil icon in the sound list.

• Recorded, imported or downloaded sounds are saved in the /SoniLoko/sonidos_propios folder on your device's storage (outside Downloads), and show up automatically in the sound list, below the built-in ones. You can delete them with the trash icon: when confirming you can choose whether to also delete the file from the device (on by default) or just remove it from the list, keeping the file. This requires granting SoniLoko full storage access from Settings.

• That same folder is used by default when exporting or importing your configuration, though the picker always lets you choose another location too.

• Turn on "Sound preview" in Settings to hear each sound as you select it in the list.

• "Allow long sounds" makes long clips play all the way through; turn it off to cap sounds at a maximum duration (pick a preset or a custom one).

• "Simultaneous sounds" is on by default, letting sounds overlap (including repeated taps on the same button); turn it off so a new sound always stops whatever is currently playing.

• Swipe sideways on the board to switch kits; the name briefly appears in the speaker.

• The top menu lists the built-in kits and, below them, your own kits: use "New kit…" to build one from scratch by adding buttons one by one, and "Manage my kits" to rename, edit (add more buttons or change existing ones) or delete them. While you're editing the board's sounds, that same menu entry turns into "Save current kit", which saves your changes to the active kit and exits edit mode.

• "Game mode" turns the board into a Simon-says game: repeat the sequences of buttons that light up and sound, growing longer each round. You get 3 lives; level and lives are shown inside the speaker. Leave anytime with the exit button.

• Every button tap includes a short vibration; turn it off in Settings with "Vibrate on tap" (on by default). It works even in silent/vibrate mode.

• Adjust language, master volume, extra gain (with a safety limit), color theme and the SoundBox Fx parameters from Settings.

• Export or import your whole configuration (including your kits and images) as a .json file.

• Under "Custom sounds" you can export all your own sounds (recorded, imported or downloaded) as a .zip to back up or share, and import a .zip to add sounds to that folder.
Are you sure you want to close SoniLoko?
Drag to move, pinch to zoom
Tap the background color you want to remove…
You haven\'t saved any custom kits yet.
This will delete the kit \"%1$s\". This action can\'t be undone.
SoniLoko needs microphone permission to record sounds.
Boosts volume beyond 100%, with a safe limit to protect the speaker.
This will remove \"%1$s\" from your own sounds. This action can\'t be undone.
Also delete the file from the device
Plays the sound when you select it in the list, before assigning it to the button.
SoniLoko needs full storage access to create the /SoniLoko folder where your recorded/imported sounds and backups are stored.
Adjust the start and end to remove silence or keep just a section.
When on, long sounds play all the way through. When off, they\'re cut off at the maximum duration.
Lets multiple sounds play at once, including repeated taps on the same button. When off, each new sound stops the previous one.
A short vibration when you tap a button. Works even in silent/vibrate mode.
To record and import sounds, and save your configuration in the /SoniLoko folder, the app needs microphone and storage access permission. You can grant them now or do it later from Settings.
Couldn\'t complete that. Check your connection.
If set, it\'s tried first when downloading an online sound; if it doesn\'t respond, the built-in method is used. Leave blank to disable it.
Export your recorded/imported/downloaded sounds as a .zip to back up or share, or import one to add them to this folder.
Export the images you\'ve assigned to buttons as a .zip to back up or share, or import one to add them to this folder.
Add to the current ones, or replace them?
A new version (%1$s) of SoniLoko is available on GitHub. Download and install it?
Save or restore all your settings, buttons and custom kits as a .json file.
Checks whether a newer version of SoniLoko has been published on GitHub.
You already have the latest version.
Where do you want to save it?
